Scenario: Write one 32-bit word to a fixed address
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test build_phase creates env and agent with UVM_ACTIVE. 2. apply_reset() waits for rst_n=1. 3. tc001_single_write_seq.start(env.agent.seqr). 4. do_write(0x100, 0xCAFEBABE, 4'b1111) creates seq_item via uvm_create. 5. Driver gets_next_item(), drives AWVALID+AWADDR. 6. Driver drives WVALID+WDATA+WSTRB simultaneously. 7. Slave asserts AWREADY then WREADY. 8. Slave writes mem[0x040]=0xCAFEBABE. 9. Slave asserts BVALID with BRESP=OKAY. 10. Driver asserts BREADY, captures BRESP. 11. seq_item_port.item_done() called. 12. Monitor captures AW+W+B handshakes, writes to AP. 13. Scoreboard check_write: updates expected_mem[0x100]=0xCAFEBABE, verifies BRESP=OKAY. 14. Coverage samples cg_operation::write_op and cg_address_range::low.
Expected_Result: BRESP=OKAY. Scoreboard PASS. No UVM_ERROR. pass_count=1 fail_count=0

Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test builds env, apply_reset(). 2. tc002_single_read_seq.start(env.agent.seqr). 3. do_read(0x100) creates seq_item with op=AXI_READ. 4. Driver drives ARVALID+ARADDR. 5. Slave asserts ARREADY, fetches mem[0x040] (value=0 unless pre-loaded). 6. Slave asserts RVALID with RDATA=mem[0x040] and RRESP=OKAY. 7. Driver asserts RREADY, captures RDATA and RRESP. 8. item_done() called. 9. Monitor captures AR+R handshakes, AP.write(item). 10. Scoreboard check_read: compares item.rdata vs expected_mem[0x100]. 11. expected_mem[0x100] defaults to 0x0 if never written.
Expected_Result: RDATA=0x00000000. RRESP=OKAY. Scoreboard PASS. No UVM_ERROR

Scenario: Write then read same address to verify data is stored correctly
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test builds env, apply_reset(). 2. tc003_write_read_seq.start(env.agent.seqr). 3. Sequence body: do_write(0x200, 0xDEADBEEF, 4'b1111). 4. Driver drives write; slave stores mem[0x080]=0xDEADBEEF. 5. Monitor captures write, scoreboard updates expected_mem[0x200]=0xDEADBEEF. 6. Sequence continues: do_read(0x200). 7. Driver drives read; slave returns mem[0x080]=0xDEADBEEF. 8. Monitor captures read, scoreboard check_read: RDATA=0xDEADBEEF vs exp=0xDEADBEEF. 9. Both scoreboard checks pass.
Expected_Result: Read returns exactly 0xDEADBEEF. Scoreboard PASS both txns. No UVM_ERROR

Scenario: Write to N consecutive word-aligned addresses in order
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test sets seq.num_transactions=20. 2. Sequence loops i=0 to 19: do_write(i*4, 0xA0000000|i, 4'b1111). 3. For each: driver drives AW+W+B. 4. Slave stores mem[i]=0xA0000000|i. 5. Monitor captures each write transaction. 6. Scoreboard updates expected_mem for each of 20 addresses. 7. All 20 BRESP verified as OKAY. 8. pass_count=20 fail_count=0.
Expected_Result: 20 writes complete. All BRESP=OKAY. Scoreboard PASS all 20. No assertion failures
Coverage_Link: cg_address_range (all 3 bins with enough N); cg_wstrb::b_all

Scenario: Read from N consecutive word-aligned addresses in order
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Pre-condition: run TC004 first (same seed/addresses) OR slave mem defaults to 0. 2. Test sets seq.num_transactions=20. 3. Sequence loops i=0 to 19: do_read(i*4). 4. Driver drives AR+R for each. 5. Slave returns mem[i] for each. 6. Monitor captures each read transaction. 7. Scoreboard checks: RDATA vs expected_mem. 8. If TC004 not run, expected_mem defaults to 0 and slave returns 0 - both match.
Expected_Result: 20 reads complete. All RRESP=OKAY. Scoreboard PASS all 20

Scenario: N fully randomized write transactions using SV constraints
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test sets seq.num_transactions=20. 2. Sequence loops: do_rand_write() calls uvm_create then item.randomize() with {op==AXI_WRITE}. 3. Constraints ensure addr[1:0]==00, addr in [0x000:0xFFC], wstrb!=0. 4. Each write driven by driver via master clocking block. 5. Slave applies byte strobes and stores result. 6. Monitor captures, scoreboard updates expected_mem with strobe-aware write. 7. All BRESP verified as OKAY.
Expected_Result: 20 random writes complete. Zero scoreboard failures. Zero assertion failures
Coverage_Link: cg_wstrb (multiple bins hit randomly); cg_data_pattern; cg_address_range

Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test sets seq.num_transactions=20. 2. Sequence loops: do_rand_read() creates item, randomize with {op==AXI_READ}. 3. Each read driven at randomized address. 4. Slave returns mem[addr[11:2]] (initialized to 0 unless prior writes). 5. Monitor captures, scoreboard compares RDATA vs expected_mem (or 0 default). 6. All RRESP verified as OKAY.
Expected_Result: 20 random reads complete. All RDATA match expected. Zero UVM_ERROR

Scenario: N random transactions with 50/50 read/write distribution
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test sets seq.num_transactions=20. 2. Sequence loops: do_rand_item() creates item, item.randomize() (no op constraint). 3. op dist {AXI_WRITE:=50, AXI_READ:=50} constraint gives ~50% each. 4. Writes update both slave memory and scoreboard expected_mem. 5. Reads compare slave output vs scoreboard expected_mem. 6. Monitor captures all 20 transactions. 7. Coverage samples cg_cross_addr_op for all address/op combinations.
Expected_Result: 20 mixed transactions. All scoreboard checks pass. Zero assertion failures. Coverage bins filled

Scenario: Apply second reset while AXI bus is idle; verify recovery
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test run_phase overrides base. 2. apply_reset() called - waits for initial rst_n=1. 3. Idle for 20 cycles. 4. force vif.rst_n=0 for 8 cycles (second reset). 5. Monitor AST_RST01-05 check: AWVALID/WVALID/ARVALID/BVALID/RVALID all deassert within 1 cycle of rst_n=0. 6. release vif.rst_n; wait for rst_n=1. 7. tc009_reset_idle_seq.start(): do_write(0x004, 0xABCD1234). 8. Write completes successfully post-reset.
Expected_Result: All RST assertions pass. Post-reset write succeeds. BRESP=OKAY. No hang

Scenario: Inject reset while transactions are being driven; verify recovery
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. apply_reset() for initial reset. 2. Fork: (a) pre_seq.start(): tc010_reset_txn_seq drives 5 random items. (b) After 3 cycles, force vif.rst_n=0 for 5 cycles mid-transfer. 3. join both forks. 4. Slave BFM responds to negedge rst_n by resetting outputs. 5. Driver detects rst_n=0 via wait condition in run_phase. 6. release vif.rst_n; wait for recovery. 7. post_seq.start(): 5 new transactions post-reset. 8. Verify no simulation deadlock.
Expected_Result: No simulation hang. RST assertions pass. Post-reset transactions complete. No UVM_FATAL
Assertions_Covered: AST_RST01-05; All channel assertions on post-reset traffic

Scenario: N consecutive writes with zero inter-transaction gaps
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test build_phase sets cfg.inter_txn_delay=0. 2. seq.num_transactions=20. 3. Sequence loops: do_write(0x400+i*4, 0xB2B00000|i, 4'b1111). 4. Driver sees inter_txn_delay=0, starts next write immediately after BREADY completes. 5. No idle cycles between transactions. 6. Monitor captures all 20 back-to-back writes. 7. Scoreboard verifies all 20 addresses correct. 8. AST_AW01 exercises back-to-back AWVALID assertion.
Expected_Result: 20 back-to-back writes complete without gaps. All BRESP=OKAY. No assertion failures

Scenario: N consecutive reads with zero inter-transaction gaps
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test build_phase sets cfg.inter_txn_delay=0. 2. seq.num_transactions=20. 3. Sequence loops: do_read(0x400+i*4). 4. Driver starts next read immediately after RREADY completes. 5. Monitor captures all 20 reads. 6. Scoreboard verifies all 20 RDATA (addresses in mid range). 7. Slave memory at 0x400+ may be 0 unless TC011 ran first.
Expected_Result: 20 back-to-back reads complete. All RRESP=OKAY. Scoreboard PASS all

Scenario: Exercise all 15 non-zero write strobe values and verify byte accuracy
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Sequence loops s=1 to 15: do_write(s*4, 0xFF000000|s, s[3:0]). 2. Each write uses unique WSTRB from 4'b0001 to 4'b1111. 3. Driver drives WSTRB correctly via clocking block. 4. Slave applies strobe: only strobed bytes written to mem[]. 5. Scoreboard applies same strobe logic to expected_mem. 6. After each write: do_read(s*4) to verify byte-accurate storage. 7. Scoreboard compares RDATA vs expected_mem for each address. 8. Repeat 15 times covering all strobe values.
Expected_Result: All 15 WSTRB values exercised. Byte-accurate write verify passes all 30 checks. cg_wstrb 100% covered

Scenario: Write and read to address beyond 4KB slave memory
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Sequence: do_write(0x00010000, 0xBADADDR0, 4'b1111). 2. Driver drives AWADDR=0x10000. 3. Slave detects word_idx=16384 >= 1024, prints warning, but still completes BRESP=OKAY. 4. Monitor captures write. Scoreboard sees addr>0xFFC, skips data check, increments pass_count. 5. do_read(0x00010000). 6. Slave returns 0xDEADBEEF for OOR address. 7. Scoreboard check_read: addr>0xFFC, skips data check, increments pass_count.
Expected_Result: Transactions complete without hang or UVM_FATAL. Warnings printed. Scoreboard skips OOR check. No assertion failures
Coverage_Link: cg_address_range (coverage note: addr[11:10]=11 so high bin)

Scenario: 1000 random read-write transactions covering full address/data space
Steps_to_Achieve: 1. Test build_phase forces num_transactions=1000 if <100. 2. tc015_stress_seq.start(env.agent.seqr). 3. Loop 1000x: do_rand_item() with full constraint set. 4. Writes update slave memory and expected_mem. 5. Reads compare against expected_mem. 6. Progress logged every 100 transactions. 7. Monitor captures all 1000. Scoreboard checks all 1000. 8. Coverage samples 1000 transactions -> fills all bins. 9. All 29 assertions monitored throughout.
Expected_Result: 1000/1000 scoreboard checks pass. Zero UVM_ERROR. Zero assertion failures. Coverage >=95% overall
Coverage_Link: ALL covergroups at high coverage; cg_wstrb 100%; cg_cross_addr_op 100%
